pattern: Each index defines its own window reaching back at most nums[i] steps, so accumulating a running prefix sum lets every one of those window sums be read off directly instead of re-summed from scratch.
hints: For a given index i, the window always ends at i and starts at max(0, i - nums[i]) -- figure out that start index first.
Precompute a prefix-sum array so the sum of any range [start, i] is a single subtraction instead of a loop.
Add up the window sum for every index from 0 to n-1 to get the final total.

edge_inputs.description: every window value is huge so every start clamps to 0
every window value is 1, so only two elements ever contribute per window

stress.description: random arrays across the full length and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
